question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Parenting and Family Support Strategy.
answer_text: My Department remains committed to continuing to work with other departments to conclude the development of the strategy. Work is intended to recommence in 2023.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether an inspection of the beech trees on Bregagh Road is planned; and (ii) what steps will be taken to ensure the integrity and conservation of the trees.
answer_text: My Department is currently engaging an arboriculturalist to assess the condition of the trees at Bregagh Road and any trees or branches deemed to represent a risk to road safety will be reported directly to the landowners. It is anticipated that the survey will provide recommendations / management proposals that seek to ensure the integrity and conservation of the trees and that in time the report can be shared with the relevant landowners.	Should you have any further questions on matter, this please contact DfI Roads Northern Division at: dfiroads.northern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what action is planned to clear the blocked drain gully on the Ballinlea Road, Ballymoney.
answer_text: My Department has programmed a drainage scheme aimed at resolving this localised flooding issue opposite No.147 Ballinlea Road, Armoy.  It is anticipated this work will commence later this month.Should you have any further questions on this matter, please contact DfI Roads Northern Division at: dfiroads.northern@infrastructure-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether Education Authority school bus drivers are subject to enhanced disclosure certificates prior to commencing employment.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that all EA bus drivers are required to have an Access NI enhanced disclosure check prior to commencing their employment as a school bus driver.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for her assessment of what actions need to be taken to end the use of letting fees in the private rental market in NI.
answer_text: The Commission on Disposals of Land Order 1986 prohibits letting agents from charging a tenant any fee for a service that should be paid by the landlord, and my joint communique, with the Finance Minister, reiterated that this practice should cease.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the plans for a new school building for the Mary Queen of Peace Primary School in Glenravel.
answer_text: Mary Queen of Peace Primary School was announced to advance in planning in January 2013 to facilitate the amalgamation of the former St Mary’s Primary School, Cargan and Glenravel Primary School, Martinstown. The school is currently operating on a split site using both former school sites.An Integrated Consultant Team, Hamilton Architects, was appointed in February 2018 to design and manage the project. A RIBA Stage 3 (developed design) submission was received in the Department in January 2020 and subsequently approved after review by the Department’s Professional Advisors.A preferred site was chosen in Dunaghy Wood, Glenravel and outline agreement reached to acquire the lands. A planning application for a new school build was approved in April 2021. Planning approval for improvement works to Skerrywater Bridge, required by the Department for Infrastructure to accommodate improved access to the site, was approved in February 2020.Formal acquisition of the site has not taken place yet due to a number of legal matters that require resolution. The site acquisition must be completed before the project can move to the next stage, which will be the procurement of an Integrated Supply Team to construct the new build.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what actions are being taken to alleviate the backlog of driving licence applications and driving tests in North Antrim.
answer_text: I can advise that the Driver & Vehicle Agency (DVA) is not experiencing any notable delays in processing applications for first provisional driving licence applications and adding provisional entitlement to an existing licence.  However, where a driver has declared that they suffer from a medical condition that may impact their ability to drive safely, further information may be requested from the driver and their medical practitioner in relation to their condition and they may be referred to our medical advisors in the Occupational Health Service (OHS) for an assessment on their medical fitness to drive.  The time taken to determine an application where a medical condition needs to be investigated depends on the medical assessment required.I can also confirm that there are currently delays in processing fitness to drive referrals. The OHS has faced challenges brought about by increased demand upon its services, limited resources, and competing priorities.  Officials in both business areas continue to liaise to prioritise and process the most urgent cases.  Officials are also engaging on options for the future provision of fitness to drive medical assessments.  In relation to driving tests, the DVA has, and continues, to work hard to increase its capacity to help meet the high demand for driving tests for all customers.  The DVA has 94 full time and dual role staff capable of conducting driving tests across its network of test centres, and is in the process of completing an external recruitment competition for 16 new full time driving examiners.  To date 12 new driving examiners have been appointed from this competition and training will continue until all new driving examiner posts are filled.  In addition to the recruitment of additional examiners, the DVA continue to offer driving tests on a Saturday and at certain centres on Sundays and bank holidays, where it is suitable to do so without compromising the integrity of the test.  Overtime is also being used to rota off-shift dual role driving examiners to provide further capacity and to provide cover for scheduled driving tests, where due to a variety of unforeseen reasons such as sick absence or the requirement to self-isolate, driving examiners are unable to attend work. The DVA is releasing Category B (private car) driving test appointments five months in advance with all test centres set to release driving test appointments on the first working day of each month and additional test slots will also be released as resources continue to become available.Due to the constantly changing position, the DVA’s advice to customers is to keep checking the booking system for availability as additional appointment slots are added when resources become available.  If slots are not initially available at a customer’s preferred test centre, they may wish to consider booking a test at another centre.I remain committed to minimising disruption and ensuring DVA’s plans for the restoration of services are

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 3094/22-27, whether the forthcoming Housing Executive survey on the extent of second homes and the impact on the housing market and the sustainability of local communities will include detailed disaggregated data on the use and purpose of second homes.
answer_text: The detailed methodological approach including the extent that the data could be disaggregated is still under development, but for the purposes of the research, second homes would be defined to include holiday homes and short term lets and it is anticipated that the project would seek to provide insights on the use and purpose of second homes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when will the outstanding Strategic Housing Market Analysis Reports be released for the (i) Ballymena; and (ii) Causeway Coast Housing Market Areas, including the (a) Mid and East Antrim; and (b) Causeway Coast and Glens local authorities.
answer_text: The Housing Executive expect to publish the outstanding Strategic Housing Market Analysis Reports covering the Northern (Causeway, Ballymena), Mid-Western (Fermanagh and Omagh; Cookstown and Dungannon) and South Eastern (Craigavon Urban Area and Newry) areas on its website on Friday 7th October 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what actions are being taken to address waste water treatment works that have reached capacity in Armoy, Dervock, Mosside, Stranocum, Grange, Cargan and Martinstown.
answer_text: The management of wastewater infrastructure across the North is an operational matter for NI Water. You should therefore, direct your question to NI Water at waterline@niwater.com.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action is being taken across Bushmills to ensure longstanding residents are not excluded from the local property market due to the surge in purchases of second homes and homes for tourist rental across the area.
answer_text: My Department does not have the remit to intervene in property transactions in the private housing market. This may be an area that Local Councils could consider as part of their wider planning and Local Development Planning process. In terms of the work on my draft Housing Supply Strategy, the need for improved housing-related data collection and analysis has been identified and forms part of the ongoing work on its development. One project which it is anticipated will help address this information gap is currently being scoped out by the Housing Executive. While, the detailed specification for the research has yet to be finalised, in broad terms the research will aim to assess the extent of second homes and their effect on the housing market and the sustainability of local communities and services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action is being taken across Ballintoy to ensure longstanding residents are not excluded from the local property market due to the surge in purchases of second homes and homes for tourist rental across the area.
answer_text: My Department does not have the remit to intervene in property transactions in the private housing market. This may be an area that Local Councils could consider as part of their wider planning and Local Development Planning process. In terms of the work on my draft Housing Supply Strategy, the need for improved housing-related data collection and analysis has been identified and forms part of the ongoing work on its development. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action is being taken across Portballintrae to ensure longstanding residents are not excluded from the local property market due to the surge in purchases of second homes and homes for tourist rental across the area.
answer_text: My Department does not have the remit to intervene in property transactions in the private housing market. This may be an area that Local Councils could consider as part of their wider planning and Local Development Planning process. question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action is being taken to address the housing crisis in (i) coastal towns; and (ii) areas of outstanding natural beauty across NI.
answer_text: The Housing Supply Strategy will provide a long-term framework to create a housing system that can supply homes across all tenures to meet housing needs and demands. This will include addressing the key supply barriers, such as access to funding, land and infrastructure.  Options are being considered for the final Strategy in the absence of a functioning Executive and an agreed budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action is being taken across Ballycastle to ensure longstanding residents are not excluded from the local property market due to the surge in purchases of second homes and homes for tourist rental across the area.
answer_text: My Department does not have the remit to intervene in property transactions in the private housing market. This may be an area that Local Councils could consider as part of their wider planning and Local Development Planning process. In terms of the work on my draft Housing Supply Strategy, the need for improved housing-related data collection and analysis has been identified and forms part of the ongoing work on its development. One project which it is anticipated will help address this information gap is currently being scoped out by the Housing Executive. While the detailed specification for the research has yet to be finalised, in broad terms the research will aim to assess the extent of second homes and their effect on the housing market and the sustainability of local communities and services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the register of attendance for young people availing of Education Other Than At School is included in each referring school’s overall attendance register.
answer_text: If a pupil has been referred for Education Other Than At School (EOTAS) and remains enrolled with their main school then dual registration applies. The Main school is required to record attendance for the sessions when a Main “Dual Registered” pupil should be attending the Main School, using the appropriate attendance code, In addition, the Main school should ensure arrangements are in place where the Host School notifies them of any pupil absences for sessions which the pupil should be attending the Host school. Attendance codes should be amended as necessary by the Main school, to take account of information received from the Host school. In some cases a student may have been expelled and therefore the EOTAS centre becomes the main school (with an Enrolment Status of Single Registration). In this case, the school must enter a date of leaving in SIMS for the pupil and forward the Common Transfer File to the relevant EOTAS centre.Guidance covering this issue is provided to schools in the following DE Circulars:Circular 2016/22 - Guidance Enrolment Status Circular 2021/16 - Attendance Guidance & Absence Recording

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many pupils excluded from school at primary level have not been offered a placement at an Education Other Than at School setting, within the last five years.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised no primary school pupil has been expelled from school in the last 5 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many pupils excluded from school at a post-primary level have not been offered a placement at an Education Other Than at School setting, within the last five years.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised this information is not currently held by the Authority.  I can confirm that the EA Education Welfare Service will support all pupils expelled from school to find an alternative school placement.  Where the EA’s Post Primary Behaviour Support and Provisions are made aware of an expulsion a place at an Education Other Than at School setting will be offered.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many young people have been (i) suspended; and (ii) expelled from Education Other Than at School placements at (a) primary; and (b) post-primary school levels, within the last five years.
answer_text: Education Otherwise Than at School (EOTAS) settings are not grant-aided schools and as such there is no legal basis to suspend or expel a pupil.  Any suspension required within EOTAS is issued by the young person’s registered school and therefore this data would be recorded under the school.  EOTAS do not expel pupils so there is no data held on this

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many post-primary school aged children and their parents have availed of the school exclusion appeals process within the last 10 years.
answer_text: The Education Authority has confirmed the number of expulsion appeals for post-primary aged children which have been heard during the last 8 school years are:  School YearAppeals Heard2014/201582015/201642016/2017122017/201802018/2019192019/2020102020/202162021/20225Prior to the establishment of the Education Authority on 1 April 2015 expulsion appeals were arranged regionally by the legacy Education and Library Boards.  Information on expulsion appeals for the school years 2012/2013 and 2013/2014 are not therefore readily available.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for her assessment of the number of young people that have been informally excluded from (i) primary; and (ii) post-primary schools, within the last five years.
answer_text: The Department does not hold any data on the number of pupils informally excluded from schools in Northern Ireland. DE Circular 2021/04 sets out the current legislative and statutory duty on Board of Governors, schools and employing authorities on the requirements for suspensions and expulsions.  The Circular reaffirms schools must not send a pupil out of school during the school day, due to behavioural issues, without recording this as a suspension, nor may schools reduce the minimum hours under instruction for any pupil, as set out in the Primary Schools (General) Regulations (NI) 1973 and the Secondary Schools (Grant Conditions) Regulations (NI) 1973, without following the procedures for applying temporary exceptions in accordance with Circular 1990/45.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many pupils have been excluded from primary schools without first receiving a suspension, since 2019.
answer_text: The Schools (Suspension and Expulsion of Pupils) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 states that “a pupil may only be expelled from a school after a period of suspension”.  My Department does not therefore collect data on the number of pupils that have been expelled without first being suspended.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many young people are currently attending Education Otherwise Than At School on a (i) full-time; and (ii) part-time basis, broken down by primary and post-primary levels.
answer_text: The number of young people currently attending Education Otherwise Than At School (EOTAS), on a full-time and part-time basis is broken down by primary and post-primary in the table below.This table also provides details of pupils being educated through Exceptional Teaching Arrangements, i.e. pupils that are unable to attend their mainstream school due to long term illness.Primary EOTAS CentresPost Primary EOTAS CentresExceptional Teaching Arrangements (ETA)Full Time-28654 (group provision Post Primary)Part Time27394.5 hours per week community based Exceptional Teaching Arrangements - 295 Post Primary pupils35 Primary pupils

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what measures are in place to mitigate the use of encouraged absences at (i) primary; and (ii) post-primary school level.
answer_text: The Department does not recognise the term “encouraged” absences,The Department’s attendance strategy “Miss School = Miss Out” published in 2016 and its associated policies and case studies on effective practice, highlight the importance of regular attendance at school. All schools have their own attendance policy and monitor pupil’s attendance on an-going basis. These measures are of vital importance as children recover from the impact of the pandemic.The EA Education Welfare Service (EWS) works with young people, schools and parents to facilitate positive school attendance.  The service will also support schools in complying with the coding of absences in line with DE Circulars.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many post-primary pupils are currently placed or have been placed on reduced timetable arrangements, in the last three years.
answer_text: Timetabling arrangements are a matter for schools.  My Department does not hold data on individual pupil timetables.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what changes have been made to the legislative framework governing the use of school exclusion since 1995.
answer_text: There have been a number of changes made to the legislative framework governing the use of school suspension and expulsion since 1995 as follows: Schools (Suspension and Expulsion of Pupils) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998.Schools (Expulsion of Pupils) (Appeal Tribunals) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998.Articles 86 (1) and (2) of the Education (NI) Order 1998 Education (Northern Ireland).Article 34 of the Education (Northern Ireland) Order 2006.My Department has also been undertaking a review of existing pupil suspension and expulsion arrangements and I will be considering the findings and recommendation of this work in the coming weeks when I receive the project board’s report.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what is the cost to the Education Welfare Service of (i) suspending; and (ii) expelling a pupil from post-primary school.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that it does not record or tally Education Welfare Service costs relating to pupil suspensions and expulsions.  Each pupil’s situation is different and the time Education Welfare Officers spend supporting them depends on many variables.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many pupils have been excluded from post-primary schools in Northern Ireland without first receiving a suspension, since 2019.
answer_text: The Schools (Suspension and Expulsion of Pupils) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 states that “a pupil may only be expelled from a school after a period of suspension”.  My Department does not therefore collect data on the number of pupils that have been expelled without first being suspended.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what are the costs to the Education Welfare Service for (i) suspending; and (ii) expelling a pupil from primary school.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that it does not record or tally Education Welfare Service costs relating to pupil suspensions and expulsions.  Each pupil’s situation is different and the time Education Welfare Officers spend supporting them depends on many variables.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education what efforts have been made to understand the experiences of school exclusion from the perspective of excluded young people over the last 10 years.
answer_text: My Department has been undertaking a review of pupil suspension and expulsion arrangements which has included engagement with young people.  I will be considering the findings and recommendation of this work in the coming months. My Department has also engaged with representatives from Queens’ University Belfast who are working on the UK wide Political Economies of School Exclusion (PolESE) project which is considering the future impacts on excluded children, and will be considering the findings of this report when finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the steps his Department is taking to identify mitigations to allow existing home care dog boarding providers to operate while legislation is prepared and brought forward to update the Animal Boarding Establishments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1974.
answer_text: It is acknowledged that complying with existing licensing requirements can be difficult for dog home boarding businesses. The Department has not, therefore, required businesses undertaking this activity to cease trading but works closely with operators to encourage them to meet licencing conditions where they are able to do so.My Department is committed to reviewing and updating the licensing system for animal boarding establishments, including those that provide boarding from homes, during this mandate. To this end, my officials are presently planning a schedule of animal welfare related legislation to be progressed during this mandate. This will include action on this matter.Please be assured, however, that, in the meantime, any dogs cared for in a home setting continue to be protected by the Welfare of Animals Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 which provides enforcement agencies with substantial powers to act in situations where an animal is subject to unnecessary suffering in any setting.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education how many post-primary pupils have been excluded from school in the last two years, either on a temporary or permanent basis and have been at home without any education provision.
answer_text: The two most recent years’ data available relate to the academic years 2018/19 and 2019/20. In 2018/19, 4,549 compulsory school age pupils were suspended (temporarily excluded) from primary schools, post-primary schools and special schools and 30 were expelled (permanently excluded). In 2019/20, 3,342 compulsory school age pupils were suspended and 11 were expelled.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Regional Stadia Development Programme and the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: In terms of the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer, the challenges created by not having a functioning Executive means I cannot bring new proposals for endorsement and agreement as is required.	 	I met with representatives from IFA and NIFL, and have agreed to work with them, and other stakeholders, to progress it.		I remain committed to moving this Programme forward

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the implementation of the recommendations in the Review of the Use of Restraint and Seclusion in Educational Settings report.
answer_text: The Department’s review of the use of restraint and seclusion in educational settings has concluded.  The report on the findings and recommendations of the review was published on 25 March 2022. My officials are now taking forward the 6 recommendations for change, including publication of new guidance in the 2022/23 school year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the steps his Department is taking to review and update the Animal Boarding Establishment Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1974 to recognise home care dog boarding.
answer_text: My Department is committed to reviewing and updating the licensing system for animal boarding establishments, including those that provide home care dog boarding, during this mandate.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs
